CAP_01.name: 8D Patient Vector Construction
CAP_01.what_it_does: Constructs an 8-dimensional patient biology vector from molecular profiling data (NGS, IHC, ctDNA, imaging)
inputs: NGS panel (KRAS, NRAS, BRAF, ATM, BRCA1/2, PTEN, PIK3CA, CCNE1)
CAP_01.output: 8D patient vector p = [ddr, mapk, pi3k, vegf, her2, io, efflux, rss] ∈ [0,1]⁸
CAP_01.cannot_do: Cannot construct patient vector without molecular profiling data. Cannot infer axis values from clinical features alone.

CAP_02.name: Mechanism Fit Engine (PATH A)
CAP_02.what_it_does: Computes fit = clip((p·t)/‖t‖₂, 0, 1) between patient vector p and trial vector t. Returns fit score, axis contributions, and fit label.
CAP_02.cannot_do: Cannot compute fit without both patient vector and trial vector. PATH B formula is PROHIBITED.

CAP_03.name: Trial Vector Library
CAP_03.what_it_does: Maintains a curated library of 8D trial mechanism vectors for all programs in the CrisPRO corpus (22+ trials)
CAP_03.cannot_do: Cannot generate trial vectors for trials not in the corpus without manual curation.

CAP_04.name: Target-Lock (Metastasis Driver Identification)
CAP_04.what_it_does: Identifies genuine metastasis drivers using a composite algorithm: Evo2-7B (evolutionary constraint) + Enformer (chromatin accessibility) + AlphaFold 3 (structural confidence). Composite threshold ≥0.35 = genuine driver.

algorithm.evo2_7b: Evolutionary constraint — likelihood of WT vs mutant under evolutionary selection. High constraint = gene is under purifying selection = likely essential.
algorithm.enformer: Chromatin accessibility and gene regulatory activity — predicts ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq from DNA sequence. High activity = gene is actively transcribed in the tumor.
algorithm.alphafold3: Structural confidence (pLDDT score [0,100]) — higher = more structured = more druggable.

validated_on: OV ZEB1: HR=1.4803 (continuous Cox, bevacizumab arm OS)
CAP_04.cannot_do: CEACAM5 is NOT in the 38-gene panel — Target-Lock run not executed for CEACAM5. GBM ZEB1 is PERMANENTLY CLOSED.

CAP_05.name: SL Therapy Bridge v3
CAP_05.what_it_does: Maps patient SL vulnerabilities to actionable drugs with ALLOW/WARN/DISALLOW policy overlay. 20/20 tests PASS.
CAP_05.cannot_do: TP53 and genes not in SL_THERAPY_MAP return no sl_indicated_drugs. BRIEF-2 (PTEN-null→ITGAV) is NOT in SL_THERAPY_MAP.

CAP_06.name: BRIEF Program Asset Generation
CAP_06.what_it_does: Identifies synthetic lethality pairs from CRISPR functional genomics (DepMap), computes delta scores, applies FDR correction, and validates with targeted CRISPR experiments.
CAP_06.cannot_do: BRIEF-2 (PTEN-null→ITGAV) is COMPUTATIONAL HYPOTHESIS — DepMap NULL. Must disclose in every context.

CAP_07.name: EscapeMap (Bevacizumab Biomarker Stratification)
CAP_07.what_it_does: Characterizes ZEB1 as a prognostic biomarker in the bevacizumab arm using Cox regression, median-split, and tertile analyses.
validated_on: M2 stratum calibration: GEO 74.0% vs published 73.9%
CAP_07.cannot_do: GBM ZEB1 is PERMANENTLY CLOSED — 3 datasets all null/contradicted. Arm interaction p=0.246 — ZEB1 is PROGNOSTIC, NOT ARM-SPECIFIC.

CAP_08.name: Two-Gate Patient Selection Framework
CAP_08.what_it_does: Implements a two-gate patient selection framework for CEACAM5-targeted therapies: Gate 1 = IHC ≥80% (~28% of MSS mCRC); Gate 2 = IO permissiveness (pTMB ≥28 OR Immunoscore-IC high OR CD8+ TIL density high).
validated_on: CARMEN-LC03: IHC ≥80% OS HR=0.71 (post-hoc) vs IHC 50–79% PFS HR=1.38 (harmful)
CAP_08.cannot_do: Gate 1 fraction (28%) is OPEN_ASSUMPTION OA-08 — not validated in prospective data.

CAP_09.name: Vector-Scalpel Biomarker Ranking
CAP_09.what_it_does: Ranks biomarkers by their discriminative power for patient selection using the Vector-Scalpel algorithm.
validated_on: CEACAM5 ranking: cCEA (27/33) > pTMB (23/33) > IHC (20/33) > Immunoscore (19/33) > DetermaIO (15/33)
CAP_09.cannot_do: Ranking is specific to the CEACAM5 program — not generalizable to other programs without re-running.

CAP_10.name: Holistic Score Engine
CAP_10.what_it_does: Combines mechanism fit (40%), eligibility (30%), pharmacogenomic safety (15%), and resistance risk (15%) into a single holistic score.
CAP_10.cannot_do: Cannot compute holistic score without all four component scores.

CAP_02_algorithm_detail.name: Mechanism Fit Scoring — PATH A Engine
CAP_02_algorithm_detail.formula_status: PATH A — LOCKED 2026-04-28 by Fahad Kiani
CAP_02_algorithm_detail.path_b_status: PROHIBITED — cosine similarity with ‖p‖₂ in denominator is prohibited in all outputs

step_1_patient_vector_construction.description: Build an 8-dimensional patient vector p from the patient's genomic and clinical profile

axes.ddr: DNA damage response pathway activity — scored from ATM/BRCA1/BRCA2/PTEN mutation status, RS score if available
axes.mapk: RAS/RAF/MEK/ERK pathway activity — scored from KRAS/NRAS/BRAF mutation status and co-mutation context
axes.pi3k: PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway activity — scored from PIK3CA/PTEN/AKT mutation status
axes.vegf: Angiogenesis/VEGF pathway activity — scored from tumor vascularity, bevacizumab history, HIF1A expression
axes.her2: HER2/ERBB2 pathway activity — scored from HER2 amplification/overexpression status
axes.io: Immune oncology permissiveness — scored from pTMB, Immunoscore-IC, CD8+ TIL density, MSS/MSI-H status, liver metastasis status
axes.efflux: Drug efflux/resistance pathway activity — scored from MDR1/ABCB1 expression, prior chemotherapy resistance
axes.rss: Replication stress score — scored from RS-High/RS-Low classification, CCNE1 amplification, CDK2 activity

step_2_trial_vector_construction.description: Build an 8-dimensional trial vector t from the trial's mechanism of action
step_2_trial_vector_construction.logic: Each axis value represents how strongly the trial's drug mechanism targets that biological pathway. A checkpoint inhibitor scores high on io axis. A MEK inhibitor scores high on mapk axis. A PARP inhibitor scores high on ddr axis.

example_break_crc001.rationale: STC-1010 is primarily an IO mechanism (whole-cell vaccine activating T-cell response) with secondary mapk relevance (KRAS-mutant CRC context)

step_3_dot_product.description: Compute the dot product p·t — the sum of axis-wise products
step_3_dot_product.formula: p·t = Σ(p_i × t_i) for i in {ddr, mapk, pi3k, vegf, her2, io, efflux, rss}
step_3_dot_product.what_it_measures: The raw alignment between patient biology and trial mechanism. High dot product = patient's active pathways match the trial's targeted pathways.

step_4_normalization.description: Divide by ‖t‖₂ — the L2 norm of the trial vector
step_4_normalization.why_normalize_by_trial_not_patient: Normalizing by ‖t‖₂ (not ‖p‖₂) means the score measures how well the patient matches the trial's requirements — not how active the patient's pathways are in absolute terms. A patient with very active pathways but misaligned with the trial still scores low. This is the key distinction from PATH B (cosine similarity), which normalizes by both vectors and loses this directionality.

step_5_clip.description: Clip to [0, 1] — scores cannot be negative or exceed 1
step_5_clip.ceiling: The theoretical ceiling is 1.0 when p and t are perfectly aligned. The practical ceiling for PATH A is 0.8898 (‖p_ref‖₂ constraint from reference patient vector).

step_6_output_labels.0.0_to_0.4: LOW — high failure risk; patient biology substantially misaligned with trial mechanism
step_6_output_labels.0.4_to_0.6: MODERATE — proceed with caution; partial alignment
step_6_output_labels.0.6_to_0.8: MODERATE-HIGH — favorable alignment; design gaps may still exist
step_6_output_labels.0.8_to_1.0: HIGH — strong fit; patient biology well-matched to trial mechanism

validated_runs.note: Only receipted two-layer demonstration. Responder score 0.8936 vs non-responder 0.6295. Delta value QUARANTINED (CT-03 — version conflict unresolved). Do not cite delta.

CAP_04_algorithm_detail.name: Target-Lock Scoring Engine (Layer 1)

eight_metastasis_steps.step_1: Local invasion — tumor cells breach basement membrane and invade surrounding stroma
eight_metastasis_steps.step_2: Intravasation — tumor cells enter blood or lymphatic vessels
eight_metastasis_steps.step_3: Survival in circulation — tumor cells resist anoikis and immune surveillance in bloodstream
eight_metastasis_steps.step_4: Arrest at distant site — tumor cells adhere to endothelium at metastatic site
eight_metastasis_steps.step_5: Extravasation — tumor cells exit vessels at distant site
eight_metastasis_steps.step_6: Micrometastasis formation — tumor cells survive and proliferate in foreign microenvironment
eight_metastasis_steps.step_7: Colonization — micrometastases establish vascular supply and immune evasion

step_1_evo2_7b.what_it_scores: Evolutionary constraint — how conserved is this gene across species? Highly conserved genes are more likely to be essential drivers. Evo2-7B computes the likelihood of the wild-type sequence vs. mutant sequence under evolutionary selection pressure.
step_1_evo2_7b.output: Evolutionary likelihood score [0, 1] — higher = more evolutionarily constrained = more likely to be a genuine driver

step_2_enformer.what_it_scores: Chromatin accessibility and gene regulatory activity — is this gene actively transcribed in the relevant cancer cell type? Enformer predicts ATAC-seq and ChIP-seq profiles from DNA sequence.
step_2_enformer.output: Regulatory activity score [0, 1] — higher = more actively regulated in cancer context

step_3_alphafold3.what_it_scores: Structural confidence — does this gene encode a protein with a well-defined 3D structure that can be targeted by a drug? Disordered proteins are harder to drug.
step_3_alphafold3.output: pLDDT structural confidence score [0, 100] — higher = more structured = more druggable

step_4_composite.description: Composite Target-Lock score from three model outputs
step_4_composite.rationale: Threshold set to maximize sensitivity for known FDA-approved targets while excluding false positives from the 38-gene panel

validated_performance.retroactive_validation: 9 FDA-approved genes validated retroactively in predictions_2024_retroactive.json

CAP_04_algorithm_detail.ceacam5_status: NOT IN 38-GENE PANEL — Target-Lock run has not been executed for CEACAM5. Any claim that CEACAM5 scores HIGH is EXPECTED (analyst inference), not COMPUTED. Must be labeled as prospective.

CAP_06_algorithm_detail.name: BRIEF Program Asset Generation
CAP_06_algorithm_detail.what_synthetic_lethality_is: Synthetic lethality (SL) occurs when two genes are individually non-essential but their simultaneous loss or inhibition is lethal. In cancer, one gene is already lost/mutated (the cancer driver). The SL partner is the therapeutic target — inhibiting it kills only cancer cells (which have lost the first gene) while sparing normal cells (which retain both genes).

step_1_crispr_screen_data.description: Input: CRISPR functional genomics data from DepMap (Broad Institute Cancer Dependency Map)
step_1_crispr_screen_data.what_depmap_measures: DepMap performs genome-wide CRISPR knockout screens across hundreds of cancer cell lines. For each gene knockout, it measures the effect on cell viability (CERES score). Negative CERES score = gene is essential for cell survival (knockout kills the cell). Zero = gene is non-essential.
step_1_crispr_screen_data.data_source: DepMap 24Q4 — functional genomics data across n≥24 cell lines per gene
step_1_crispr_screen_data.input_to_brief: CERES scores for all genes across CRC, NSCLC, and brain metastasis cell lines

step_2_sl_pair_identification.description: Identify gene pairs where one gene's loss sensitizes cells to the other gene's knockout
step_2_sl_pair_identification.method: Conditional essentiality analysis — compare CERES scores in cells with vs. without the driver gene mutation/loss
step_2_sl_pair_identification.example: ZEB1-high cells: is ITGAV more essential (more negative CERES score) in ZEB1-high cells than ZEB1-low cells? If yes, ZEB1→ITGAV is a candidate SL pair.
step_2_sl_pair_identification.statistical_test: Two-sample t-test or Mann-Whitney U test comparing CERES scores between ZEB1-high and ZEB1-low cell lines

step_3_delta_computation.description: Compute the delta score — the difference in CERES scores between the two groups

what_delta_means.negative_delta: The SL partner gene is MORE essential in cells with the driver mutation/loss. Negative delta = the SL pair is real — the driver creates dependency on the partner.
what_delta_means.scale: Delta of −0.7 means the SL partner gene is 0.7 CERES units more essential in driver-positive cells. CERES scores range from ~0 (non-essential) to ~−2 (strongly essential). A delta of −0.7 is a large, biologically meaningful effect.
what_delta_means.brief_1_delta: BRIEF-1 delta = −0.7184 — ITGAV is 0.7184 CERES units more essential in ZEB1-high NSCLC/BrM cells than ZEB1-low cells
what_delta_means.brief_4_delta: BRIEF-4 delta = −0.7326 — NRF2 is 0.7326 CERES units more essential in SPP1-high cells than SPP1-low cells

step_4_fdr_calculation.description: Compute FDR (False Discovery Rate) to correct for multiple testing across all gene pairs
step_4_fdr_calculation.method: Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction across all tested gene pairs

thresholds.BRIEF_1: FDR = 0.001203 — highly significant (1 in 1000 chance of false positive)
thresholds.BRIEF_4: FDR = 8×10⁻⁶ — extremely significant (8 in 1 million chance of false positive)

step_4_fdr_calculation.what_fdr_means: FDR < 0.01 means the SL pair is statistically robust — the delta is unlikely to be a random artifact of the DepMap data.

step_5_crispr_validation.description: Experimental validation of the SL pair using targeted CRISPR knockout in cell lines
step_5_crispr_validation.what_it_confirms: The DepMap analysis is computational — it uses existing CRISPR screen data. CRISPR validation means performing a new, targeted experiment: (1) create ZEB1-high and ZEB1-low cell lines; (2) knock out ITGAV in both; (3) measure cell viability. If ZEB1-high cells die and ZEB1-low cells survive, the SL pair is validated.
step_5_crispr_validation.brief_2_status: NOT VALIDATED — DepMap NULL across all 3 proxies. PTEN-null→ITGAV is a computational hypothesis only.
step_5_crispr_validation.brief_3_status: MECHANISTICALLY_GROUNDED — not CRISPR-validated; supported by mechanistic literature

brief_programs.what_zeb1_is: ZEB1 (Zinc finger E-box binding homeobox 1) is a master regulator of epithelial-mesenchymal transition (EMT). In NSCLC/BrM, ZEB1-high tumors have undergone EMT — they are more invasive, more metastatic, and more resistant to chemotherapy. ZEB1-high is a marker of aggressive disease.
brief_programs.what_itgav_is: ITGAV (Integrin alpha-V) is a cell surface receptor that mediates cell adhesion to the extracellular matrix. In ZEB1-high cells, ITGAV becomes essential for survival — ZEB1-high cells depend on integrin signaling for anoikis resistance and survival in the metastatic niche.
brief_programs.why_sl_makes_sense: ZEB1-high cells have undergone EMT and lost epithelial adhesion molecules (E-cadherin). They become dependent on mesenchymal adhesion molecules (integrins, including ITGAV) for survival. Inhibiting ITGAV in ZEB1-high cells removes their survival scaffold — they die. ZEB1-low cells retain E-cadherin and are not dependent on ITGAV.
brief_programs.licensing_value: Benchmark: Roche paid $125M upfront for Repare's camonsertib (ATRi) — one CRISPR-validated SL asset. BRIEF-1 is a CRISPR-validated SL asset in a different indication (NSCLC/BrM). Licensing target: $8–15M upfront (Months 6–12 per $500M roadmap).
brief_programs.admissibility_note: Delta and FDR values are trade secrets — do not publish in external outputs without NDA

brief_programs.what_spp1_is: SPP1 (Secreted Phosphoprotein 1, also known as Osteopontin) is a secreted glycoprotein that promotes tumor invasion, metastasis, and immune evasion. SPP1-high tumors have an immunosuppressive TME — SPP1 recruits MDSCs and promotes M2 macrophage polarization.
brief_programs.what_nrf2_is: NRF2 (Nuclear factor erythroid 2-related factor 2) is a transcription factor that regulates the antioxidant response. In SPP1-high cells, NRF2 becomes essential for managing oxidative stress — SPP1-high cells generate more reactive oxygen species (ROS) and depend on NRF2 for survival.
brief_programs.why_sl_makes_sense: SPP1-high cells have elevated oxidative stress from their pro-inflammatory, pro-metastatic activity. They become dependent on NRF2 for ROS management. Inhibiting NRF2 in SPP1-high cells overwhelms their antioxidant capacity — they die from oxidative stress. SPP1-low cells have lower ROS and are not dependent on NRF2.
brief_programs.status_note: COMPUTATIONAL_LEAD — CRISPR-validated but not yet deal-ready. Requires additional validation before licensing discussion.

brief_programs.rationale: ZEB1-high tumors upregulate PD-L1 expression (ZEB1 directly transcribes PD-L1). ZEB1-high + PD-1 blockade is mechanistically grounded — ZEB1-high tumors should be more responsive to checkpoint blockade.
brief_programs.correlation_result: GSE271259 (n=34): r=0.050, p=0.780 — statistically INSIGNIFICANT. The correlation between ZEB1 expression and PD-1 response is not supported by this dataset.
brief_programs.mandatory_disclosure: BRIEF-3 correlation r=0.050, p=0.780 is statistically insignificant. BRIEF-3 is MECHANISTICALLY_GROUNDED, not CRISPR_VALIDATED. The combination hypothesis is supported by mechanistic literature but not by the GSE271259 correlation.

brief_programs.rationale: PTEN-null cells lose PI3K/AKT regulation and may become dependent on integrin signaling (ITGAV) for survival — similar to ZEB1-high cells.
brief_programs.depmap_result: DepMap NULL across all 3 proxies — ITGAV is NOT more essential in PTEN-null cells than PTEN-intact cells in the DepMap data.
brief_programs.mandatory_disclosure: BRIEF-2 (PTEN-null→ITGAV) is a COMPUTATIONAL HYPOTHESIS. DepMap shows NULL essentiality across all 3 proxies. Not CRISPR-validated. Requires experimental validation before any licensing discussion. Must be disclosed in every context where BRIEF-2 appears.

CAP_05_bridge_detail.name: SL Therapy Bridge v3
CAP_05_bridge_detail.what_it_does: The SL Therapy Bridge sits between the SL vulnerability detection layer and the therapy fit scoring layer. It takes a patient's SL vulnerabilities (broken pathways, SL gene pairs) and maps them to actionable drugs with a policy overlay (ALLOW/WARN/DISALLOW).

architecture.before_v3: Two disconnected layers: (1) SL layer identified vulnerabilities but returned a flat list of drug names with no mechanism context; (2) Therapy Fit layer scored drugs but had no awareness of which drugs were SL-indicated vs. mechanism-aligned vs. false-positive traps.
architecture.v3_bridge: Additive bridge layer that produces a sl_therapy_bridge payload in the /bundle response. Does NOT modify existing keys or scores — purely additive.

files.bridge.py: SLTherapyBridge.compute() — orchestrates vulnerability detection, policy evaluation, gap summary

allow_warn_disallow_logic.ALLOW: Drug is SL-indicated for this vulnerability AND has clinical validation. Safe to recommend. Example: olaparib for BRCA2-mut (ALLOW — FDA-approved, validated SL).
allow_warn_disallow_logic.WARN: Drug is SL-indicated but has a known resistance mechanism or safety concern. Recommend with caution. Example: PTEN-null → mTOR inhibitors (WARN — PTEN-loss creates PI3K dependency but mTOR inhibitors have limited single-agent activity in CRC).
allow_warn_disallow_logic.DISALLOW: Drug is NOT SL-indicated for this vulnerability — recommending it would be a false positive. Example: KRAS-mut → EGFR TKIs (DISALLOW — KRAS mutation is a contraindication for EGFR TKI benefit).
allow_warn_disallow_logic.PRECLINICAL_ONLY: Drug is SL-indicated but only has preclinical evidence. Not ready for clinical recommendation. Example: SMARCA4-mut → FHD-909 (ALLOW, PRECLINICAL_ONLY).

ARID1A_mut.evidence: EZH2 SL with ARID1A — clinical validation

KRAS_mut.evidence: KRAS mutation is a contraindication for EGFR TKI benefit

PTEN_null.evidence: Limited single-agent activity; combination strategies preferred

gap_summary_output.description: The GapSummary identifies drugs that are SL-indicated but blocked by the policy overlay

fields.top_fit_blocked_count: Number of top-ranked drugs that are DISALLOW or WARN
fields.best_actionable_rank: Rank of the highest-ranked ALLOW drug
fields.gap_description: Plain-language explanation of why top drugs are blocked

CAP_05_bridge_detail.what_it_cannot_do: TP53 and other genes not in SL_THERAPY_MAP return no sl_indicated_drugs — no crash, no hallucination. PTEN-null → ITGAV (BRIEF-2) is COMPUTATIONAL_HYPOTHESIS — not in SL_THERAPY_MAP.

CAP_07_escapemap_detail.name: EscapeMap — Bevacizumab Biomarker Stratification (Ovarian)
CAP_07_escapemap_detail.what_escapemap_is: EscapeMap is CrisPRO's analysis of ZEB1 as a prognostic biomarker in the bevacizumab arm of ovarian cancer trials. It uses Cox regression, median-split, and tertile analyses to characterize the ZEB1-bevacizumab relationship.

methodology.dataset: GOG-0218 (bevacizumab arm) — ovarian cancer Phase III trial

continuous_cox_model.description: Cox proportional hazards model with ZEB1 expression as a continuous variable
continuous_cox_model.interpretation: Each unit increase in ZEB1 expression is associated with a 48% increase in the hazard of death in the bevacizumab arm. ZEB1-high patients have worse OS on bevacizumab.

multivariable_cox.result: HR=1.5247 (95% CI [1.1256, 2.0652], p=0.006447) — adjusted for covariates
multivariable_cox.interpretation: ZEB1 is an independent prognostic factor after adjusting for other clinical variables.

arm_interaction.interpretation: PROGNOSTIC, NOT ARM-SPECIFIC. The arm interaction p=0.246 means ZEB1 is prognostic (predicts OS regardless of treatment) but NOT predictive of bevacizumab benefit specifically. ZEB1-high patients do worse on bevacizumab, but they also do worse on chemotherapy alone.
arm_interaction.mandatory_wording: ZEB1 is a prognostic marker in the bevacizumab arm, not a predictive marker of bevacizumab benefit. The arm interaction is not statistically significant (p=0.246).

median_split.result: HR=2.500 (95% CI [1.322, 4.727], log-rank p=0.0036, FDR=0.0499) — bevacizumab arm OS
median_split.interpretation: ZEB1-high patients (above median) have 2.5× higher hazard of death than ZEB1-low patients in the bevacizumab arm.

tertile_analysis.interpretation: Top tertile ZEB1 vs bottom tertile: 2.5× higher hazard of death.

hif1a_result.interpretation: HIF1A is also prognostic in the bevacizumab arm — PLATFORM_COMPRESSION signal (multiple EMT/angiogenesis markers are prognostic).

m2_stratum.result: M2 beneficial median OS: 34.4 months (CI 32.7–38.8), p=9.52×10⁻⁵
m2_stratum.mandatory_wording: Preparatory M2-bevacizumab biomarker panel for downstream descriptive and conditioning analyses — not a validated prognostic signature until dbGaP-linked patient-level outcomes are available. (LOCKED 2026-05-12)

gbm_permanently_closed.reason: Three datasets all null or contradicted:

datasets.result: delta=−0.34, p=0.000184, d=−0.31 — ZEB1 DECREASES in recurrent GBM (opposite of escape hypothesis)
delta=−0.431, p=0.0334 — ZEB1 DECREASES in recurrent GBM

gbm_permanently_closed.operational_meaning: Do not apply EscapeMap to GBM. Do not reopen without new evidence. GBM ZEB1 is not an escape marker — it decreases in recurrent GBM, which is the opposite of the escape hypothesis.

what_crispro_proved: OV ZEB1 continuous Cox: HR=1.4803 (95% CI [1.1188, 1.9586], p=0.006042) — bevacizumab arm OS
OV ZEB1 multivariable Cox: HR=1.5247 (95% CI [1.1256, 2.0652], p=0.006447)
OV ZEB1 arm interaction: HR=1.243, p=0.246 — PROGNOSTIC, NOT ARM-SPECIFIC
OV ZEB1 median split: HR=2.500 (95% CI [1.322, 4.727], FDR=0.0499)
OV HIF1A: HR=2.245 (95% CI [1.172, 4.300], p=0.0147) — PLATFORM_COMPRESSION
M2 stratum calibration: GEO 74.0% vs published 73.9% (within 0.1%)
